mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are uncorrelated with rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of quarters, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks and factory slowdowns, triggering short-term negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances following an industrial production drop, or the signal fails during stable growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a tightening labor market forces monetary policy to remain restrictive, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive when both inflation and employment rise, or the interaction effect reverses sign during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with GDP growth over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 300,000 signals an overheating labor market that raises recession fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of months with payroll additions above 300,000, or the threshold effect disappears in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production alongside rising retail sales confirms synchronized economic strength, supporting positive equity returns over the next month during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The synchronized signal yields negative forward returns in > 50% of months, or predictive accuracy drops to zero during stagflationary environments.
